qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated database reconfiguration via unprotected admin/install.php
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unauthenticated access to admin/install.php in maintenance mode and missing admin directory protection, while the advisory concerns SOAP API authentication bypass in api/soap/mc_api.php::mci_check_login() caused by improper type checking of the password parameter on MySQL.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SOAP API password type-checking root cause. The advisory patch only changes api/soap/mc_api.php::mci_check_login() to enforce nullable string parameters and normalize null password handling before authentication, whereas the sole qualifying finding targets the admin installer maintenance-mode path.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-phrq-pc6r-f6gh describes authentication bypass through the SOAP API on MySQL due to improper type checking of the password parameter.

Patch b349e5c890eeda9bd82e7c7e14479853f8a30d9f modifies api/soap/mc_api.php::mci_check_login(), adding ?string types for username and password and using the normalized password in api_token_validate() and auth_attempt_script_login().

The only final qualifying report, admin-install-unauth-access, lists affected entrypoints GET/POST /admin/install.php, GET /admin/check/index.php, and GET /admin/upgrade_unattended.php, with code locations in admin/*.php and core.php.
The qualifying finding's root cause is MANTIS_MAINTENANCE_MODE skipping authentication plus lack of admin directory web-access protection, not SOAP password type coercion on MySQL.
